# Default example

This example demonstrates the basic usage of the CAF Naming utility module.

This module is a pure data module that:
- Provides CAF-compliant resource type abbreviations
- Generates deterministic unique strings (similar to ARM `uniqueString`)
- Sanitizes input values for use in resource names
- Generates zero-padded instance numbers for sequential naming

**Note:** This module does not deploy any Azure resources.

```hcl
terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.9, < 2.0"

  required_providers {
    random = {
      source  = "hashicorp/random"
      version = "~> 3.5"
    }
  }
}

# This is a utility module that provides CAF naming data.
# It does not deploy any Azure resources.
module "naming" {
  source = "../../"

  enable_telemetry = var.enable_telemetry
  # Instance numbering for sequential naming
  instance_number  = 1
  instance_padding = 3
  # Sanitize inputs for use in resource names
  # Output is lowercase alphanumeric only
  sanitize_inputs = {
    project     = "My-Project!"
    environment = "PROD"
    team        = "Platform-Team"
  }
  # Unique string generation (similar to ARM uniqueString)
  # Use a seed to generate deterministic hash values
  unique_string_seed = ["my-subscription-id", "my-resource-group", "eastus"]
}







```

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD033 -->
## Requirements

The following requirements are needed by this module:

- <a name="requirement_terraform"></a> [terraform](#requirement\_terraform) (>= 1.9, < 2.0)

- <a name="requirement_random"></a> [random](#requirement\_random) (~> 3.5)

## Resources

No resources.

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD013 -->
## Required Inputs

No required inputs.

## Optional Inputs

The following input variables are optional (have default values):

### <a name="input_enable_telemetry"></a> [enable\_telemetry](#input\_enable\_telemetry)

Description: This variable controls whether or not telemetry is enabled for the module.  
For more information see <https://aka.ms/avm/telemetryinfo>.  
If it is set to false, then no telemetry will be collected.

Type: `bool`

Default: `true`

## Outputs

The following outputs are exported:

### <a name="output_abbreviations_sample"></a> [abbreviations\_sample](#output\_abbreviations\_sample)

Description: Sample of CAF-compliant resource type abbreviations

### <a name="output_example_storage_account_name"></a> [example\_storage\_account\_name](#output\_example\_storage\_account\_name)

Description: Example storage account name using abbreviation and unique hash

### <a name="output_hash_variants"></a> [hash\_variants](#output\_hash\_variants)

Description: Hash variants of different lengths

### <a name="output_instance"></a> [instance](#output\_instance)

Description: Zero-padded instance number

### <a name="output_rules_sample"></a> [rules\_sample](#output\_rules\_sample)

Description: Sample naming rule for key\_vault

### <a name="output_sanitized"></a> [sanitized](#output\_sanitized)

Description: Sanitized input values

### <a name="output_unique_string"></a> [unique\_string](#output\_unique\_string)

Description: 13-character deterministic hash (same length as ARM uniqueString)

## Data Collection

The software may collect information about you and your use of the software and send it to Microsoft. Microsoft may use this information to provide services and improve our products and services. You may turn off the telemetry as described in the repository. There are also some features in the software that may enable you and Microsoft to collect data from users of your applications. If you use these features, you must comply with applicable law, including providing appropriate notices to users of your applications together with a copy of Microsoft’s privacy statement. Our privacy statement is located at <https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=824704>. You can learn more about data collection and use in the help documentation and our privacy statement. Your use of the software operates as your consent to these practices.
